$comment: Reference map of Benchling EntryLink.type tokens -> how the packager treats them. Source of truth for `type` enum: test/openapi.yaml (EntryLink schema, 18 tokens). Categories mirror docker/src/entry_references.py (LINK_TYPE_CATEGORY); test_entry_references.py asserts they agree. Endpoints / id prefixes / events per issue #389. See issues #143, #389, #68.

categories.entity: Registry entity; GET-by-id and v2.entity.registered event. Packageable.
categories.inventory: Packageable via GET-by-id; no webhook events.
categories.reference: Packageable; has its own create/update webhook events.
categories.metadata: GET works but low value to package as a record.
categories.not_packageable: No read API exists; keep the webURL as a reference only.
categories.uncertain: Endpoint depends on tenant API version (e.g. v2-alpha); verify before relying.
categories.external: Plain http(s) hyperlink ('link' type); no Benchling ID.
categories.unknown: Type not in the known enum; surfaced rather than dropped.

link_types.notes: Locations do not have a webURL.
Verify against the deployed tenant's API version before relying on it.
Linkable but no GET-by-id endpoint in the stable spec.
No read API; Insights/analytics artifact reachable only via the Warehouse. Keep webURL deep link.
Legacy alias of sql_dashboard; no read API.
Plain external hyperlink; id omitted, webURL is the literal URL.

not_inline_linkable.$comment: Resources that are NOT EntryLink types -- they cannot appear in links[]. They reach an entry via structured note parts / inventory tables instead.
